title: Surrendered to Christ. Actually Free.
caption:
The most surrendered man in the room is also the freest one in it. Not in spite of it. Because of it.

Two guys think they're free and neither one is. One answers to no one, calls it strength. Really it's a city with no walls. Whatever he can't say no to just walks in and takes the room. "Like a city whose walls are broken down is a man who lacks self-control." — Proverbs 25:28

The other never says no to anyone else, calls it easygoing. He hasn't told his own flesh no long enough to find out if he could. Soft isn't the same as free.

Here's the part nobody tells him: whoever you obey is already your master, whether you say his name out loud or not. Sin, or Christ. No neutral gear. "You are slaves of the one whom you obey." — Romans 6:16

That's what makes Galatians 5 freedom different from every other kind. Christ didn't set you free to do whatever you want. He set you free to finally do what's right, even when it costs you. Free enough to stay when leaving is easier. Free enough to close the door before anyone's watching.

Save this for the next time an old impulse tries to tell you it's actually in charge.

#faith #selfcontrol #christianmen #manofgod #galatians #discipline #freedom #scripture #christianity
theme: He runs his life. His impulses don't control him, because Christ does.
